where_to_eat.name: Local neighborhood restaurants and cafes
where_to_eat.description: Buenos Aires offers numerous traditional cafes and restaurants throughout the city. Many travelers recommend exploring the local dining scene and eating 'a ton' during their visits, with classic plazas featuring old-style cafes perfect for experiencing authentic Buenos Aires culture.

into_the_wild.name: El Palmar National Park
into_the_wild.description: Located in the Paraná region near Buenos Aires, this national park features distinctive Yatay palm trees and scenic landscapes, making it a great nature destination for day trips from the city.

into_the_wild.name: Perito Moreno National Park
into_the_wild.description: Located in Patagonia accessible from Buenos Aires, this park features stunning glaciers and natural landscapes that visitors describe as 'one of the most impressive' in Argentina.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Buenos Aires city boulevards and plazas
gram_worthy_spots.description: The city features wide boulevards, ornate facades, and classic plazas with elegant European-style architecture that creates photogenic moments, particularly during rainy weather walks.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Historic cafes
gram_worthy_spots.description: Buenos Aires is filled with old, atmospheric cafes that offer perfect Instagram-worthy locations with classic Argentine charm and vintage aesthetics.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Rosario
off_the_beaten_path.description: A nearby city visited by travelers exploring beyond Buenos Aires proper, offering an alternative perspective to the capital with its own character and attractions.
